title: Athlete Non‑Dairy Milks
description: Which non‑dairy milks are high‑protein and suitable for athletes needing ample protein during training?
narrative: The user seeks nutritionally relevant, high‑protein alternative milks for athletic performance, requiring info on protein content, bioavailability, flavor, and suitability for training; relevance requires evidence‑based nutrition data, product comparisons, and athlete review insights.

title: Bluetooth Speaker Selection
description: Which features distinguish high‑quality Bluetooth speakers for both home and mobile use?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive guidance on selecting Bluetooth speakers, focusing on sound reproduction, battery life, connectivity options (Bluetooth 5.x, Wi‑Fi, AirPlay), build quality, durability, and cost. Relevant sources should discuss comparative analyses, expert reviews, consumer testimonials, technical specifications, and trade‑offs between performance, portability, and price.

title: Rural Digital Literacy
description: What are effective strategies for increasing digital literacy among adults in rural areas?
narrative: The user is a public policy analyst looking for evidence-based interventions that improve digital skills for rural adults. Relevance criteria include studies, program reports, or evaluations that detail outcomes, methodologies, cost implications, and sustainability of community-driven approaches or technology access initiatives.

title: Dynamic Pricing Law
description: Which federal legal provisions regulate dynamic pricing and off‑peak electricity usage for residential consumers?
narrative: The user is a residential utility consultant who must understand the full spectrum of federal statutes, FCC rulings, and relevant court decisions that govern the implementation of time‑of‑use tariffs and off‑peak scheduling for home customers. Relevance criteria include documents that specifically address federal legal frameworks for dynamic pricing, identify regulatory agencies involved, and discuss precedent-setting court rulings; purely state‑level policy, generic corporate filings without legal analysis, or unrelated industry reports are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Traffic
description: How has the shift to remote work affected traffic congestion in major cities?
narrative: The user seeks recent studies, empirical data, and city‑specific case analyses that quantify changes in peak‑hour traffic volumes, average commute times, and congestion costs attributable to increased remote work adoption. Relevance requires sources that discuss measurable effects, reference relevant time frames (post‑2020), and distinguish between telecommuting trends and other confounding factors such as fuel price changes or public transit adjustments.

title: Poverty–Health Nexus
description: How does socioeconomic inequality in New Zealand affect health outcomes, and which policy measures have been studied to address these disparities?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive evidence on the link between poverty and health status in New Zealand and wants details on relevant policy proposals, interventions, and evaluation results. Documents must focus on New Zealand, discuss socioeconomic indicators, health metrics, policy analysis, and contain recent research or official reports (last 20 years).

title: AI Alignment Critiques
description: What are the main academic criticisms of current AI alignment research?
narrative: The user is looking for peer‑reviewed academic papers, policy analyses, and expert commentaries that challenge or critique standard AI alignment methods. Relevant documents should provide evidence‑based arguments, counterexamples, or methodological weaknesses rather than general summaries or promotional material.

title: Visa-Free Travel Options
description: Which countries allow visa-free entry for a 90-day stay for U.S. citizens?
narrative: The user wants a concise list of countries where U.S. passport holders can enter without a visa for up to 90 days, excluding destinations that require ESTA, eTA, or any form of pre-approval, focusing on short-term travel freedom.

title: Spastic vs. Ataxic
description: How can clinicians differentiate between spastic and ataxic dysarthria in patients presenting with speech difficulties?
narrative: A speech‑language pathologist or neurologist seeks precise clinical criteria—motor patterns, prosody, articulation accuracy, and tongue–lip control—to distinguish spastic dysarthria, characterized by muscle rigidity and harsh voice, from ataxic dysarthria, marked by irregular rhythm and jerky movements. Relevance requires evidence‑based descriptions from neurological and speech‑language literature, including case studies, neuroanatomical correlates, and acoustic analysis techniques that aid differential diagnosis.

title: Autonomous Delivery Regulations
description: What are the current federal and state regulations governing the deployment of autonomous delivery robots in U.S. urban areas?
narrative: The user, a logistics manager evaluating a new autonomous delivery system, needs a detailed overview of regulatory constraints, permitting processes, safety standards, and compliance requirements across major states to assess feasibility and timeline for deployment.

title: Phasing Out Plastics
description: What evidence-based policy interventions can local governments adopt to phase out single‑use plastics within the next five years?
narrative: The user is a municipal policy maker seeking specific, actionable strategies. Relevance requires documents that detail real‑world policy models, implementation timelines, outcome metrics, and case studies of successful plastic bans. Documents that only offer generic environmental commentary or unrelated data are not considered relevant.

title: Distributed Team Productivity Tools
description: Which collaboration tools are best suited for teams spread across multiple time zones to maintain productivity and low latency?
narrative: The user intends to discover software that supports synchronous and asynchronous communication, flexible scheduling, and real‑time collaboration while minimizing latency. Relevance is limited to systems that explicitly address multi‑time‑zone coordination, provide time‑zone aware features (e.g., scheduling assistants, shared timers), support cross‑device integration, and have proven performance in large distributed teams. Documents describing generic project management or personalization features without time‑zone consideration should be considered irrelevant. The user values comprehensive comparative analysis, cost transparency, and case studies from industries similar to theirs.

title: Indo-Pacific Fisheries Policies
description: Which policy measures implemented by Indo‑Pacific nations have proven most effective at reducing overfishing and promoting sustainable fisheries management?
narrative: The user seeks to identify specific legislation, enforcement mechanisms, and community‑based initiatives from multiple Indo‑Pacific countries, assess their impact on fish stocks and livelihoods, and compare evidence of effectiveness—emphasizing quantifiable results and case studies.

title: Urban Green Space Impact
description: What measurable effects do urban green spaces have on community well‑being in North American cities?
narrative: The user is researching urban planning and public health. They want scholarly studies, municipal reports, and case analyses that quantify outcomes such as mental health improvements, social cohesion, air quality, economic benefits, and usage patterns. Relevance is determined by studies published in peer‑reviewed journals or official city reports after 2000, focusing on North American urban areas and measuring at least one objective community well‑being metric.

title: AI Patent Challenges
description: What are the major challenges and best practices for protecting AI-generated inventions under current U.S. patent law?
narrative: The user is a legal researcher seeking detailed analysis of AI patentability, including recent cases, statutory interpretations, and guidance for drafting claims. Relevant documents should provide scholarly articles, court opinions, and regulatory guidelines that directly address AI-related patent issues.

title: Nurse Salary by State
description: What are the current salary ranges for nurse practitioners across different states?
narrative: The user is a registered nurse evaluating an advanced practice career and wants up-to-date, state‑specific salary data for nurse practitioners by specialty. Relevant documents should contain recent salary surveys, state health department statistics, or peer‑reviewed studies that provide average or median incomes, with breakdowns by specialty and geography. Documents that only discuss RN‑to‑BSN pathways, general nursing duties, or non‑salary related career advice are considered irrelevant.

title: Remote Work Productivity Metrics
description: What are the most effective quantitative metrics for measuring productivity and employee engagement in fully remote work settings?
narrative: The user is a HR manager wanting to evaluate remote teams and is looking for meaningful, evidence‑based productivity indicators beyond simple logged hours. Relevant documents should discuss studies, tools, and industry benchmarks that cover output quality, collaboration frequency, task completion rates, and employee well‑being in fully remote contexts.

title: Circular Economy Policy
description: What are the key components of national circular economy strategies in the EU?
narrative: The user seeks an overview of the main elements that governments include in circular economy roadmaps, such as waste reduction targets, recycling incentives, and product lifecycle regulations, and wants to compare how these components differ across EU member states.

title: Urban Food Systems
description: What innovative strategies exist for making city food supply chains resilient to climate change?
narrative: The user is a city‑planning researcher seeking policy, infrastructure, and community‑based approaches that strengthen urban food systems against climate shocks. Relevant documents should cover climate adaptation measures, supply‑chain redesign, local agriculture initiatives, food‑security policies, and comparative case studies of cities that have successfully implemented resilient food systems.

title: Local Tourist Assistance
description: Where and how can foreign tourists receive help from Thai locals, especially regarding cultural guidance and currency exchange regulations?
narrative: The user is interested in practical support options available to visitors in Thailand, focusing on personal interactions with locals for cultural assistance and navigating the specific restrictions on currency conversion. Relevance is judged by information that describes local help networks, language support, useful contacts or services, and official advice on exchanging money with Thai baht in both onshore and offshore contexts.

title: Microplastics Impact Study
description: Which recent peer‑reviewed studies quantify the effects of microplastics on top predator seabirds?
narrative: The user wants up‑to‑date scientific research that examines how ingestion or bioaccumulation of microplastics influences the health, behavior, and population dynamics of apex seabirds.  Relevance is defined by studies published after 2015, including both field observations and laboratory experiments, that report quantitative results and address specific seabird species and microplastic sources.

title: Urban Low‑Carbon Transit
description: What are the most effective low‑carbon public transit options for rapidly growing urban regions?
narrative: The user seeks data‑driven analysis of low‑carbon public transit systems, including electric bus fleets, bike‑sharing networks, and multi‑modal hubs, with emphasis on feasibility, cost, scalability, and policy requirements. Relevant documents should cite peer‑reviewed studies, real‑world city case studies showing measurable carbon reductions, and evaluation of financial and implementation challenges.

title: Vegan Protein Evaluation
description: Which vegan protein powders offer the best balance of complete amino acid profiles, high digestibility, and low environmental impact for athletes?
narrative: The user is a sports nutritionist wanting evidence-based guidance on selecting vegan protein supplements that deliver a complete amino acid spectrum, high digestibility scores, and minimal carbon footprint. Relevant materials should cite peer-reviewed studies, product ingredient analyses, life-cycle assessment data, and comparison tables that evaluate metrics such as PDCAAS, DIAAS, digestibility, and greenhouse gas emissions per gram of protein.

title: Influencer Metric Evaluation
description: Which metrics should brands monitor to assess the effectiveness of influencer marketing on social media?
narrative: The user wants to move beyond basic engagement figures and understand a comprehensive set of performance indicators—including reach, engagement rate, sentiment analysis, audience demographics, click‑through rates, and conversion metrics—so that they can accurately evaluate and optimize influencer campaigns. Relevant documents will discuss contemporary analytic frameworks, case studies, and toolkits for measuring influencer marketing ROI.

title: Remote Monitoring
description: How can providers implement remote monitoring for chronic heart failure to improve patient outcomes?
narrative: The user seeks practical, evidence‑based guidance on deploying remote patient monitoring systems for chronic heart failure management, including clinical protocols, technology selection, EHR integration, outcome data, and reimbursement pathways. Relevant documents should provide actionable strategies, studies demonstrating effectiveness, policy or payer information, and implementation nuances specific to chronic heart failure, while excluding generic telehealth advice or unrelated disease applications.

title: Urban Heat Mitigation
description: Which urban design interventions most effectively reduce city surface temperatures during heatwaves?
narrative: The user is an urban planner aiming to identify evidence-based strategies—such as green roofs, reflective pavements, and increased tree canopy—to mitigate heat island effects. Relevance criteria include studies with empirical temperature measurements, cost‑benefit analyses, and scalable policy recommendations.

title: Smart-Home Privacy
description: Which mechanisms do smart-home platforms offer for users to control personal data while still delivering full device functionality?
narrative: The user seeks to understand the trade‑off between data sharing required for IoT device operations and user‑controlled privacy settings. Relevant documents describe technical controls (encryption, local processing), policy frameworks (consent management, data minimization), and standards (smart‑home specifications). Documents that only cover general privacy or unrelated IoT use cases are not relevant.

title: Root Stem Cell Regulation
description: How do root stem cells contribute to the formation and patterning of vascular tissues during plant development?
narrative: The user is interested in understanding the cellular and molecular mechanisms that orchestrate root stem cell activity, particularly how it influences the differentiation and arrangement of xylem and phloem within the root. Relevant documents should provide detailed insights into the signaling pathways, transcription factors, and environmental cues that guide stem cell tissues toward specific vascular identities, including experimental studies, gene expression profiles, and comparative analyses across species.

title: Mental Health Support
description: Which free or low‑cost mental health counseling services are available in New York City for adults experiencing anxiety?
narrative: The user wants local, affordable professional support with flexible scheduling, targeting anxiety specifically, and prefers services offering sliding‑scale fees or culturally competent providers.

title: AI Ethics Guidelines
description: What are the key ethical principles and guidelines recommended by major international organizations for AI development and deployment?
narrative: The user wants authoritative international AI ethics frameworks, such as those from the EU, UNESCO, IEEE, and the UN, covering principles like fairness, accountability, transparency, privacy, and human rights. Documents should present concrete policy proposals, consensus frameworks, or regulatory draft documents, and exclude purely academic articles, technical specifications, or unrelated AI discussions.

title: Remote Work Productivity
description: How remote work arrangements influence employee productivity across different industries?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based studies comparing productivity metrics before and after remote work adoption, with a focus on industry‑specific data, longitudinal analyses, and controlled experiments. Relevant documents should be scholarly articles, industry white papers, or government reports published within the last decade that explicitly measure productivity changes attributable to remote work setups.

title: Urban Farm Best Practices
description: What are the most effective strategies for managing small‑scale urban farms in dense city environments?
narrative: The user is a city planner or entrepreneur looking for evidence‑based methods that improve yield, cut costs, and promote community engagement in urban agriculture. Relevant documents must present specific, practical approaches, include case studies or metrics, or provide policy recommendations tailored to small‑scale, city‑based farms. General agriculture or large‑scale farming guidance is not relevant.

title: Urban Microplastic Pollution
description: Identify the primary sources of microplastics in city air and evaluate effective mitigation strategies.
narrative: The user is researching environmental health impacts of microplastics in urban settings. He/she needs recent studies that quantify airborne microplastic levels, trace their origins (e.g., synthetic textiles, tire wear, packaging), and assess mitigation measures such as filtration, policy interventions, or material innovation. Relevance entails documents that provide up-to-date empirical data, source attribution, and case studies of interventions in metropolitan areas.

title: Remote Work Impact
description: What are the effects of remote work on social skills development and mental health among adolescents?
narrative: The user aims to understand how the increased prevalence of remote work environments influences the social skill acquisition and psychological well-being of teenagers, seeking evidence from longitudinal studies, expert analyses, and meta-analyses that address both positive and negative outcomes.

title: Urban Infrastructure Integration
description: What are the best practices for integrating disparate transportation and utility systems within a smart city framework?
narrative: The user seeks detailed methodologies, case studies, and standards that enable seamless interoperability among traffic control, electric grid, water management, and public safety networks, focusing on scalable architectures, data exchange protocols, and governance models.

title: Affordable Herpes Relief
description: What affordable, effective treatments or home‐care methods can help cure or control herpes simplex infection beyond expensive antiviral drugs?
narrative: The user is a patient who has tried costly antiviral prescriptions that did not cure them. They want information on low‑price or non‑pharmaceutical options—such as over‑the‑counter remedies, natural products, or clinician‑approved low‑cost treatments—that have been proven effective, with evidence or case studies showing successful outcomes.

title: Urban Planning Biodiversity
description: How does urban planning affect biodiversity in suburban areas?
narrative: The user seeks studies and data that link specific urban planning interventions to measurable changes in biodiversity within suburban contexts, including policy analysis, ecological indicators, and comparative case studies.

title: Adjustable Mortgage Default Trends
description: How did initial adjustable‑rate mortgage offers impact long‑term default rates among sub‑prime borrowers?
narrative: The user is a risk analyst researching the causal relationship between low introductory rates, subsequent rate adjustments, and borrower default patterns over multiple years. They require evidence drawn from historical loan‑level data, demographic sub‑groups, and statistical models that highlight both the magnitude and duration of default risk attributable to the adjustable‑rate structure.

title: Paris Airport to Disneyland
description: What are the most cost‑effective transportation options between Paris Charles de Gaulle Airport and Disneyland Paris, including taxi, train, and shuttle services?
narrative: The user seeks a detailed comparison of travel modes from the airport to Disneyland, focusing on total cost, travel time, convenience, and any available discounts or promotions. Relevance requires documents that present fare structures, schedule information, and practical tips for planning the trip.

title: Rainfall Runoff Dynamics
description: How does rainfall intensity influence runoff generation in rural catchments, and what factors cause non‑linear or non‑monotonic relationships?
narrative: The user is an environmental engineer researching hydrologic response. They seek studies that quantify runoff as a function of rainfall, accounting for soil saturation, land cover, terrain slope, and urbanization. Relevance includes work that uses statistical, physical, or empirical models, discusses limitations, and highlights non‑linearities.

title: Spruce Dust Asthma
description: What are the health risks of exposure to spruce dust for sawmill workers?
narrative: A user seeks up-to-date medical and industrial hygiene research on respiratory effects, especially asthma, resulting from inhalation of spruce dust in sawmill or woodworking settings. Relevance requires peer‑reviewed studies, regulatory guidelines, occupational exposure limits, and intervention strategies, not general botanical information.

title: EV Tax Incentives Impact
description: What are the current federal and state tax incentives for electric vehicle purchases and how have they changed over the past five years?
narrative: The user is researching policy options for promoting electric vehicle adoption and needs recent, authoritative information such as official tax codes, government reports, and peer‑reviewed studies that detail the tax credits available, eligibility criteria, and historical trends. Relevance is judged by whether the source cites current legislation, provides quantitative estimates of incentive amounts, or compares different states’ programs over the specified time frame. Non‑technical blogs or unrelated hobbyist discussions are considered non‑relevant.

title: Fast Food Burger Value
description: How does the pricing of double and triple cheeseburgers compare to their nutritional and umami content?
narrative: The user seeks a cost‑versus‑value analysis of fast‑food cheeseburgers, focusing on pricing, nutritional content, and umami flavor contributions; relevant documents should discuss price per patty, calorie counts, macronutrients, and glutamate/umami levels in menu items.
